extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Only r4 (IF elderly AND pregnancy, priority 50) sets rec_drug_a := true, and only r1 (IF elderly, priority 50) sets rec_drug_b := true. No rule ever sets either output to false, so the defaults (false) apply only when a rule does not fire. With elderly=true and pregnancy=true: r1 fires making rec_drug_b=true, and r4 fires making rec_drug_a=true. Both are true simultaneously, with no competing or higher-priority rule to override them. The remaining inputs only affect rec_drug_c (via r2/r3/r5) and are irrelevant; setting them false suffices. Therefore the mutual exclusion property is violated.
